title: Rearrange One Shelf Today
subtitle: A ten-minute activity for when the whole house feels wrong but redecorating is not on the cards
excerpt: A ten-minute activity for when the whole house feels wrong and a full redecorate is not coming any time soon. The shelf gives the wanting somewhere to go. The day ends with one part of the house that has been touched.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The small-act-on-the-home pattern is a staple of slow-living writing.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the whole house feels wrong, when a big redecorate is not coming any time soon, and the wanting needs somewhere small to go.

content.text: A ten-minute home practice. Pick one shelf. Rearrange it. The day ends with one part of the house that has been touched.
New to activity as a practice? Read

When the whole house feels wrong, the urge is to gut it. To redecorate the lounge, repaint the kitchen, replace the sofa. The urge is usually larger than the budget and the energy, and the urge ends in nothing being done.
One shelf is the move that fits the actual energy of a tired afternoon. The bookshelf, the kitchen open shelf, the chest of drawers in the bedroom, the windowsill in the hallway. Any shelf will do.
The body has been telling the head that the home is wrong. The head has been answering with plans the body cannot afford to do. The shelf is the smallest move that the body can finish.
A finished shelf changes what the rest of the room looks like for the next week. The eye keeps coming back to it. The house feels touched. The body has had a small win.
Pick the shelf before you start. One shelf, not a row of shelves.
Wipe the shelf with a damp cloth.
Put back only what you actually want on it. Half is allowed. Less is allowed.
What does not go back goes in a small box or a drawer. The decisions about that pile do not have to be made today.
Step back. Look at the shelf for one minute. That is the end of the practice.
Original to homemade.education. The small-act-on-the-home shape is common in slow-living writing and in any house-keeping custom that has had to work around small money and small time.
